content.summary: Hyro (hyro.ai) is a leading Responsible AI Agent Platform for healthcare, founded in 2018 by Israel Krush (CEO) and Rom Cohen (COO) at Cornell Tech. Headquartered in New York City (240 W 37th St), with an R&D office in Tel Aviv, Hyro employs approximately 150 people. The company has raised $95 million in total funding; its most recent round was a $45M strategic growth round (October 2025) led by Healthier Capital, with Norwest, Define Ventures, ServiceNow Ventures, and Bon Secours Mercy Health also participating. Hyro's platform — built on its proprietary Agentic Conversational Engine (ACE) — automates patient communications across call centers, web, SMS, and mobile using HIPAA-compliant AI agents deeply integrated with Epic EMR, Salesforce, Cisco, and other healthcare systems. It is deployed across 50+ leading U.S. health systems (including Intermountain Health, Baptist Health, and Hackensack Meridian Health), serving 30+ million patients. In June 2026, Hyro launched Care Intelligence, an analytics layer surfacing operational insights from patient interactions. Key buying signals include active post-funding expansion, a new product launch, aggressive hiring, market expansion into pharma/payers, and doubling of ARR and team size in 2024.
content.industry: Healthcare AI / Conversational AI / Digital Health SaaS
tech_stack: Proprietary ACE (Agentic Conversational Engine) — LLMs + healthcare-specific small language models
Voice AI (inbound + outbound via Proactive Px™)
content.headquarters: 240 W 37th St, 7th Floor, New York, NY 10018, USA (secondary: Tel Aviv, Israel)
content.funding_stage: Growth Round (post-Series B) — $45M strategic growth round closed October 2025
key_investors: Healthier Capital (lead — both Series B extension Dec 2024 and $45M growth round Oct 2025)
buying_signals: Actively deploying $45M growth round capital (closed Oct 2025) to expand AI agent development and market reach
New product launch: Care Intelligence analytics platform launched June 30, 2026 — new analytics/intelligence category entry
Actively hiring: posted open roles in Sales Engineering, Solution Architecture, and Support Engineering Manager (Oct 2025+)
Market expansion beyond health systems into pharmaceutical and payer segments
Doubled ARR and team size in 2024; strong growth trajectory
50+ U.S. health systems deployed (Intermountain Health, Baptist Health, Hackensack Meridian, Sutter Health, Tampa General, Prisma Health, Piedmont, Inova Health)
Strategic investment by ServiceNow Ventures signals deeper platform/workflow integration roadmap
Doubled company valuation within 10 months between the Dec 2024 Series B extension ($35M) and Oct 2025 growth round ($45M)
Named to CB Insights Digital Health 50 (2025) and Fierce Healthcare Fierce 15 (2026) — strong enterprise credibility signals
Partnership expansions with Salesforce, Cisco, and Kyruus creating integrated solution ecosystem
content.headcount_estimate: ~150 employees (About Us page states 150; LinkedIn shows 51–200 range with 188 profiles)
